I loved this movie. Don’t listen to the professional reviewers. **Spoiler alert** I’m going to talk about the movie. The professional reviewers complain about its implausible details. But that’s its beauty. If that were the criteria for a good movie, Back To The Future would never have been made. The fact that the movie is able to sustain your attention and engage so well despite its implausibility is that ‘suspension of disbelief’ thing that all fictional movies require. That’s movie magic and this movie has it. It was never dull thanks to a fantastic director and Josh Harnetts superb acting skills. And may I say thanks to casting for bringing him back to the movies and excellent casting all around. Josh’s been off the radar for a while but when he’s in the movie he carries it. The directors close up moments when Josh’s character is contemplating are truly chilling, you want to look away. The entire cast I must say, portrayed their characters at a perfect pitch to keep stringing you along, not too alert, not too aware, until it’s needed. Great twist. This movie was a fun roller coaster night out at the movies. Please more of this and less Marvel Comics. M. Night Shyamalan is my favorite screenwriter, he keeps knocking them out of the park.

In the Unbreakable Trilogy, the main character in each movie is color coded with their own specific hue. The Overseer is always signified by the color green. Mr. Glass is associated with the color purple, and Kevin/The Beast can always be seen in and around the color yellow. This is solidified in the main poster for the movie "Glass"....So if you notice this guy, this main character in this movie "Trap" is seemingly synonymous with the color red. I like Hartnett and Shamaylan; but the premise of the police using a concert, with tens of thousands of people, to catch a killer, is just absurd.  Also, how does this work?  They are going to check everyone leaving? For what?  They have his fingerprints, DNA, what?  That's the only way they could ID someone this way.  Otherwise, they would have to know his identity or have his photo and this wouldn't need to be done.  And taking everyone's fingerprints and/or DNA leaving a concert would NEVER work in the US, where this is supposed to be (even though the detective or whomever is English).  I'm all for suspension of disbelief, but there is a limit to which it can be done, without taking you completely out of the story.  This is especially true when it is supposed to be a real world situation, not sci-fi or fantasy or just a little bit of these like Unbreakable,  Glass, and other Night movies (where it is still based in our reality and everything else works the way we would expect), still in the real world with everything else.  With this, it just makes the whole premise hard to accept, when you go too far.  Maybe future trailers or the movie itself will give a somewhat plausible explanation; but I can not, for the life of me, see how.
